topic_groups.topic: Infectious Disease Ecology and Public Health

articles.title: Scientists sound alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
articles.summary_3_sentences: Free-living amoebae are being flagged as an emerging global health concern. The report says some species can cause severe infections and may also shelter other microbes, while warming temperatures and aging water systems may aid their spread. Researchers call for better surveillance and stronger water treatment measures.

articles.title: Ancient mass grave reveals how a pandemic wiped out a city 1,500 years ago
articles.summary_3_sentences: Archaeological findings from an ancient mass grave in Jordan provide evidence of the Plague of Justinian's impact on a city. The burial pattern suggests many victims were interred rapidly over a short period. The report highlights how pandemics can reshape communities as well as cause mortality.

articles.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a virus within a common gut bacterium that may help explain links between that microbe and colorectal cancer. The work points to bacterium-virus interactions as a possible contributor to disease risk. The findings may inform future risk assessment or screening approaches, but the report does not establish causation in patients.

articles.title: Scientists discover skincare compound that kills drug-resistant bacteria
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists report that madecassic acid, a compound derived from Centella asiatica, showed activity against drug-resistant bacteria including E. coli. The summary says it targets a bacterial protein absent in humans, suggesting a potentially selective mechanism. This is an early-stage antibacterial finding rather than a proven clinical treatment.
